signals.reasoning: Active geopolitical veto and RED macro keep risk high, but BTC already flushed from 73k to 70.6k with long-liquidation characteristics, so this is not an attractive fresh short or scaled long here.

ETH has already broken lower with a fresh long-liquidation flush and rising cascade risk, but the move is extended enough under veto that chasing new downside is lower quality than waiting for structure to reset.

SOL is still weak and mechanically bearish, yet the current move already reflects a broad market flush and the event brake is active, so the cleaner Oracle stance is watch rather than force a late short.
TNSR keeps a bearish tape, but the notional liquidity is far too small and the move is already trending lower, which makes it a poor fresh entry under a veto regime.
ACE has squeeze fuel from negative funding, but the liquidity is too thin and the board is still dominated by macro risk, so the squeeze setup is not strong enough to override the veto.
DYM is down hard with negative funding, but this already looks like a damaged post-flush chart rather than an early squeeze long, and the low liquidity keeps it out of fresh-entry territory.
FARTCOIN still leans bearish on tape, yet the price/OI profile is already a long-liquidation flush after an 11% drop, so the edge on a brand-new short is weaker than it looks.
LAYER remains structurally weak, but the combination of thin liquidity, high OI-to-volume, and an already extended down day argues for patience rather than a fresh position.
USUAL has one of the more bearish mechanical scores on the sheet, but the market depth is too poor and the event veto is too strong to justify acting on that score alone.

GOAT has the most obvious squeeze fuel from negative funding and crowded shorts, but the chart is badly damaged after a 14% drop and the liquidity is too low for a clean long under current macro stress.

DASH is still weak after giving back a large multi-day move, but it has already flushed enough that the better Oracle read is no chase here; wait for either a real bounce-fail or a deeper stabilization first.
